text: Gratitude is not only the greatest of virtues, but the parent of all others.
author: Cicero

text: Gratitude turns what we have into enough.
author: Unknown

text: When I started counting my blessings, my whole life turned around.
author: Willie Nelson

text: Gratitude is the fairest blossom which springs from the soul.
author: Henry Ward Beecher

text: Enjoy the little things, for one day you may look back and realize they were the big things.
author: Robert Brault

text: Gratitude is the sign of noble souls.
author: Aesop

text: Feeling gratitude and not expressing it is like wrapping a present and not giving it.
author: William Arthur Ward

text: Gratitude is riches. Complaint is poverty.
author: Doris Day

text: Wear gratitude like a cloak, and it will feed every corner of your life.
author: Rumi

text: This is a wonderful day. I have never seen this one before.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Gratitude is when memory is stored in the heart and not in the mind.
author: Lionel Hampton

text: Acknowledging the good that you already have in your life is the foundation for all abundance.
author: Eckhart Tolle

text: Gratitude unlocks the fullness of life. It turns what we have into enough, and more.
author: Melody Beattie

text: Be thankful for what you have; you'll end up having more.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: It is not joy that makes us grateful; it is gratitude that makes us joyful.
author: David Steindl-Rast

text: Gratitude makes sense of our past, brings peace for today, and creates a vision for tomorrow.
author: Melody Beattie

text: If the only prayer you ever say in your entire life is thank you, it will be enough.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: Gratitude is the healthiest of all human emotions.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: We must find time to stop and thank the people who make a difference in our lives.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: Silent gratitude isn't much use to anyone.
author: Gertrude Stein

text: As we express our gratitude, we must never forget that the highest appreciation is not to utter words, but to live by them.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: Gratitude is a powerful catalyst for happiness. It's the spark that lights a fire of joy in your soul.
author: Amy Collette

text: Give thanks for a little and you will find a lot.
author: Hansa Proverb

text: When you are grateful, fear disappears and abundance appears.
author: Tony Robbins

text: The roots of all goodness lie in the soil of appreciation for goodness.
author: Dalai Lama

text: No one who achieves success does so without acknowledging the help of others. The wise and confident acknowledge this help with gratitude.
author: Alfred North Whitehead

text: Reflect upon your present blessings, of which every man has plenty; not on your past misfortunes, of which all men have some.
author: Charles Dickens

text: Gratitude is the memory of the heart.
author: Jean-Baptiste Massieu

text: Let us be grateful to the people who make us happy; they are the charming gardeners who make our souls blossom.
author: Marcel Proust

text: Thankfulness is the beginning of gratitude. Gratitude is the completion of thankfulness.
author: Henri Frederic Amiel

text: In ordinary life, we hardly realize that we receive a great deal more than we give, and that it is only with gratitude that life becomes rich.
author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer

text: Gratitude and attitude are not challenges; they are choices.
author: Robert Braathe

text: He is a wise man who does not grieve for the things which he has not, but rejoices for those which he has.
author: Epictetus

text: Gratitude is a currency that we can mint for ourselves, and spend without fear of bankruptcy.
author: Fred De Witt Van Amburgh

text: Piglet noticed that even though he had a Very Small Heart, it could hold a rather large amount of Gratitude.
author: A.A. Milne

text: Gratitude changes everything.
author: Unknown

text: Start each day with a grateful heart.
author: Unknown

text: There is always, always, always something to be thankful for.
author: Unknown

text: Gratitude is the wine for the soul. Go on. Get drunk.
author: Rumi

text: Appreciation is a wonderful thing. It makes what is excellent in others belong to us as well.
author: Voltaire

text: The way to develop the best that is in a person is by appreciation and encouragement.
author: Charles Schwab

text: Not what we say about our blessings, but how we use them, is the true measure of our thanksgiving.
author: W.T. Purkiser

text: When we give cheerfully and accept gratefully, everyone is blessed.
author: Maya Angelou

text: 'Thank you' is the best prayer that anyone could say.
author: Alice Walker

text: Gratitude helps you to grow and expand; gratitude brings joy and laughter into your life and into the lives of all those around you.
author: Eileen Caddy

text: The thankful receiver bears a plentiful harvest.
author: William Blake

text: Gratitude is the ability to experience life as a gift. It liberates us from the prison of self-preoccupation.
author: John Ortberg

text: Wake at dawn with a winged heart and give thanks for another day of loving.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: Some people grumble that roses have thorns; I am grateful that thorns have roses.
author: Alphonse Karr

text: Gratitude, like faith, is a muscle. The more you use it, the stronger it grows.
author: Alan Cohen

text: If you count all your assets, you always show a profit.
author: Robert Quillen

text: The more grateful I am, the more beauty I see.
author: Mary Davis

text: Gratitude is a divine emotion: it fills the heart, but not to bursting; it warms it, but not to fever.
author: Charlotte Bronte

text: Be grateful for what you already have while you pursue your goals.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: None is more impoverished than the one who has no gratitude.
author: David A. Bednar

text: Gratitude will shift you to a higher frequency, and you will attract much better things.
author: Rhonda Byrne

text: Showing gratitude is one of the simplest yet most powerful things humans can do for each other.
author: Randy Pausch

text: We can only be said to be alive in those moments when our hearts are conscious of our treasures.
author: Thornton Wilder

text: Gratitude is the most exquisite form of courtesy.
author: Jacques Maritain

text: Gratitude bestows reverence, allowing us to encounter everyday epiphanies, those transcendent moments of awe that change forever how we experience life and the world.
author: John Milton

text: Joy is the simplest form of gratitude.
author: Karl Barth

text: Nothing is more honorable than a grateful heart.
author: Seneca

text: Got no checkbooks, got no banks. Still I'd like to express my thanks. I got the sun in the mornin' and the moon at night.
author: Irving Berlin

text: Gratitude is a quality similar to electricity: it must be produced and discharged and used up in order to exist at all.
author: William Faulkner

text: Do not spoil what you have by desiring what you have not; remember that what you now have was once among the things you only hoped for.
author: Epicurus

text: Let gratitude be the pillow upon which you kneel to say your nightly prayer.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Gratitude paints little smiley faces on everything it touches.
author: Richelle E. Goodrich

text: An attitude of gratitude brings great things.
author: Yogi Bhajan

text: When you practice gratefulness, there is a sense of respect toward others.
author: Dalai Lama

text: Gratitude opens the door to the power, the wisdom, the creativity of the universe.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: Things turn out best for people who make the best of the way things turn out.
author: John Wooden

text: Be thankful for everything that happens in your life; it's all an experience.
author: Roy T. Bennett

text: It's a funny thing about life, once you begin to take note of the things you are grateful for, you begin to lose sight of the things that you lack.
author: Germany Kent

text: Count your blessings, not your problems.
author: Unknown

text: A grateful heart is a magnet for miracles.
author: Unknown

text: Gratitude turns what we have into enough, and more. It turns denial into acceptance, chaos into order, confusion into clarity.
author: Melody Beattie

text: Happiness cannot be traveled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed. Happiness is the spiritual experience of living every minute with love, grace, and gratitude.
author: Denis Waitley

text: The essence of all beautiful art, all great art, is gratitude.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Gratitude is one of the strongest and most transformative states of being.
author: Jen Sincero

text: For me, every hour is grace.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: The unthankful heart discovers no mercies; but the thankful heart will find, in every hour, some heavenly blessings.
author: Henry Ward Beecher

text: Learn to be thankful for what you already have, while you pursue all that you want.
author: Jim Rohn

text: Gratitude is more of a compliment to yourself than someone else.
author: Raheel Farooq

text: We often take for granted the very things that most deserve our gratitude.
author: Cynthia Ozick

text: If you want to turn your life around, try thankfulness. It will change your life mightily.
author: Gerald Good

text: Gratitude is a mark of a noble soul and a refined character. We like to be around those who are grateful.
author: Joseph B. Wirthlin

text: True forgiveness is when you can say, 'Thank you for that experience.'
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: Gratefulness is the key to a happy life that we hold in our hands, because if we are not grateful, then no matter how much we have we will not be happy.
author: David Steindl-Rast

text: The deepest craving of human nature is the need to be appreciated.
author: William James

text: There is a calmness to a life lived in gratitude, a quiet joy.
author: Ralph H. Blum

text: Give yourself a gift of five minutes of contemplation in awe of everything you see around you.
author: Wayne Dyer

text: I would maintain that thanks are the highest form of thought, and that gratitude is happiness doubled by wonder.
author: G.K. Chesterton

text: To speak gratitude is courteous and pleasant, to enact gratitude is generous and noble, but to live gratitude is to touch Heaven.
author: Johannes A. Gaertner

text: Gratitude is an antidote to negative emotions, a neutralizer of envy, hostility, worry, and irritation.
author: Sonja Lyubomirsky

text: God gave you a gift of 86,400 seconds today. Have you used one to say 'thank you'?
author: William Arthur Ward

text: Strive to find things to be thankful for, and just look for the good in who you are.
author: Bethany Hamilton

text: Appreciation can change a day, even change a life. Your willingness to put it into words is all that is necessary.
author: Margaret Cousins

text: When a person doesn't have gratitude, something is missing in his or her humanity.
author: Elie Wiesel

text: The struggle ends when gratitude begins.
author: Neale Donald Walsch

text: Take full account of what excellencies you possess, and in gratitude remember how you would hanker after them, if you had them not.
author: Marcus Aurelius
